title: Hedging accounts: rebalance INCREASES accumulate same-side tickets (net correct, unbounded ticket growth)
source: Live demo shakeout 2026-05-29: reduce+restore left 10 tickets (4 symbols doubled); net exposure correct, flattened+reopened clean to 6.
why: On a RETAIL_HEDGING account, the hedging-safe rebalance correctly REDUCES by closing tickets (B0091), but an INCREASE opens a NEW same-side ticket rather than enlarging the existing one (MT5 hedging has no add-to-position). get_positions nets correctly so EXPOSURE is always right, but tickets accumulate across rebalances -> margin/UI clutter and unbounded ticket growth over a multi-week run. Cosmetic/efficiency, not an exposure bug.
value_rationale: Low: exposure is always correct (reconcile nets); only ticket count grows. Matters for a long shakeout / many rebalances; a periodic flatten-reopen (already validated) is a sufficient interim mitigation.
scope: Option A: periodic flatten-reopen to consolidate tickets (close-by-ticket validated; cheap).
Option B: on hedging increases, no clean MT5 add-to-position exists -> accept N tickets/symbol + monitor margin; consolidate when ticket count per symbol exceeds a threshold.
Netting accounts are unaffected (a single position nets).

history.reason: logged from live shakeout ticket-accumulation observation
chore(ops): position-dump + emergency-flatten utilities; log B0092 (hedging ticket accumulation)
PM dispatch: bound per-symbol ticket count on hedging accounts; market-closed safe window before next rebalance

Hedging ticket-consolidation guard: max_tickets_per_symbol=3 default on MT5Broker; flatten+reopen reuses B0091 close-by-ticket; net exposure preserved exactly; netting unaffected; 5 tests + parity green (1115 pass); commit f157618
chore(hackathon-pm): mark B0092 done + iteration log (hedging ticket-consolidation guard)
